dependencies.description: ERSPAN requires IP reachability between the source switch and the remote destination collector. The ERSPAN destination IP must be routable from the source device.
dependencies.name: IP routing (ERSPAN)

dependencies.description: The SPAN destination port must have enough bandwidth to receive all mirrored traffic. Oversubscription causes packet drops and incomplete captures.
dependencies.name: Sufficient bandwidth on destination

failure_modes.description: A local SPAN session is configured but the destination port receives no mirrored packets. Packet captures on the destination port are empty. This can be caused by incorrect source port or VLAN selection, wrong direction filter (rx/tx/both), hitting the platform's session limit, or an overly restrictive ACL filter that excludes the traffic of interest.

resolutions.action: Verify source interface/VLAN and direction match the traffic you want to capture: 'show monitor session <id>'
Verify source interface/VLAN and direction match the traffic you want to capture: 'show monitor session <id>'
Verify source interface/VLAN and direction match the traffic you want to capture: 'show monitor session <id>'
Check platform session limits — remove unused sessions if at maximum
Check platform session limits — remove unused sessions if at maximum
Check platform session limits — remove unused sessions if at maximum
Remove or adjust ACL filter to include the traffic of interest
Remove or adjust ACL filter to include the traffic of interest
Remove or adjust ACL filter to include the traffic of interest
Ensure source interface is up: 'show interface <source>'
Ensure source interface is up: 'show interface <source>'
Ensure source interface is up: 'show interface <source>'
Try 'both' direction if unsure whether traffic is ingress or egress
Try 'both' direction if unsure whether traffic is ingress or egress
Try 'both' direction if unsure whether traffic is ingress or egress
On NX-OS: verify 'no shut' on the monitor session (sessions can be admin-shut)
On NX-OS: verify 'no shut' on the monitor session (sessions can be admin-shut)
On NX-OS: verify 'no shut' on the monitor session (sessions can be admin-shut)

root_causes.cause: Source port or VLAN is incorrect — mirroring the wrong traffic
Direction misconfigured — set to 'rx' but need 'tx', or vice versa
Session limit reached — platform supports a maximum number of SPAN sessions and all slots are consumed
ACL filter too restrictive — 'filter access-group' is dropping the traffic of interest
Source interface is down or in err-disabled state
SPAN destination port is in the wrong VLAN or has STP blocking
Source is a port-channel but only the logical interface was specified (some platforms require member ports)

failure_modes.scenario: SPAN session not capturing traffic — destination port shows no mirrored traffic
symptoms: Packet capture on SPAN destination port is empty
Wireshark/tcpdump shows no traffic on the monitor port
'show monitor session' shows session as operational but no counters incrementing
SPAN destination port link is up but no traffic received

failure_modes.description: An ERSPAN source session is configured but the remote destination collector receives no mirrored traffic. ERSPAN encapsulates mirrored traffic in GRE (IP protocol 47) and sends it to a remote IP. Failures are typically caused by the ERSPAN destination IP being unreachable, GRE being blocked by ACLs or firewalls along the path, the source interface being down, or MTU issues since ERSPAN adds 50+ bytes of overhead (GRE + ERSPAN header).

resolutions.action: Verify ERSPAN destination IP is reachable: ping from the source device
Verify ERSPAN destination IP is reachable: ping from the source device
Verify ERSPAN destination IP is reachable: ping from the source device
Check for GRE/ACL blocks along the path — ERSPAN uses IP protocol 47 (GRE)
Check for GRE/ACL blocks along the path — ERSPAN uses IP protocol 47 (GRE)
Check for GRE/ACL blocks along the path — ERSPAN uses IP protocol 47 (GRE)
Verify MTU end-to-end: original frame + 50 bytes ERSPAN overhead must fit within path MTU; increase MTU or use truncation
Verify MTU end-to-end: original frame + 50 bytes ERSPAN overhead must fit within path MTU; increase MTU or use truncation
Verify MTU end-to-end: original frame + 50 bytes ERSPAN overhead must fit within path MTU; increase MTU or use truncation
Ensure ERSPAN ID matches on source and destination sessions
Ensure ERSPAN ID matches on source and destination sessions
Ensure ERSPAN ID matches on source and destination sessions
If using VRF, specify the correct VRF in the ERSPAN source configuration
If using VRF, specify the correct VRF in the ERSPAN source configuration
If using VRF, specify the correct VRF in the ERSPAN source configuration
Verify ERSPAN type compatibility (Type II vs Type III) between source and collector
Verify ERSPAN type compatibility (Type II vs Type III) between source and collector
Verify ERSPAN type compatibility (Type II vs Type III) between source and collector

root_causes.cause: ERSPAN destination IP is unreachable from the source device — no route or next-hop ARP failure
GRE (IP protocol 47) blocked by ACL or firewall along the routed path
Source interface is down or not generating traffic
MTU issue — ERSPAN adds 50+ bytes overhead (original frame + GRE + ERSPAN header), causing drops if path MTU is insufficient
ERSPAN ID mismatch between source and destination configuration
VRF misconfiguration — ERSPAN destination IP is in a different VRF than the tunnel source
Incorrect ERSPAN type — source configured as Type II but destination expects Type III, or vice versa

failure_modes.scenario: ERSPAN tunnel not delivering packets — remote capture device receives nothing
symptoms: ERSPAN source session configured but remote collector receives no traffic
Wireshark on the remote collector shows no GRE/ERSPAN packets
'show monitor session' shows ERSPAN source as operational but destination receives nothing
Intermittent ERSPAN delivery — some packets arrive, others are lost (MTU issue)

verify_commands.confirms_if: Shows ERSPAN session details — confirms if the tunnel endpoint configuration is correct
verify_commands.look_for: Check ERSPAN source session status, destination IP, ERSPAN ID, origin IP, and operational state

failure_modes.description: When multiple high-bandwidth source ports are mirrored to a single destination port, the aggregate mirrored traffic can exceed the destination port's capacity. This causes packet drops on the destination port, resulting in incomplete or misleading packet captures. For example, mirroring four 10G source ports (both directions) to a single 10G destination port can produce up to 80 Gbps of mirrored traffic against a 10G output limit.

resolutions.action: Add ACL filters to limit mirrored traffic to only the flows under investigation
Add ACL filters to limit mirrored traffic to only the flows under investigation
Add ACL filters to limit mirrored traffic to only the flows under investigation
Use 'rx' or 'tx' direction instead of 'both' if only one direction is needed
Use 'rx' or 'tx' direction instead of 'both' if only one direction is needed
Use 'rx' or 'tx' direction instead of 'both' if only one direction is needed
Reduce the number of source ports to match destination capacity
Reduce the number of source ports to match destination capacity
Reduce the number of source ports to match destination capacity
Use a higher-speed destination port (e.g., 10G/25G instead of 1G)
Use a higher-speed destination port (e.g., 10G/25G instead of 1G)
Use a higher-speed destination port (e.g., 10G/25G instead of 1G)
Use ERSPAN to distribute captures across multiple remote collectors
Use ERSPAN to distribute captures across multiple remote collectors
Use ERSPAN to distribute captures across multiple remote collectors
Enable truncation to reduce mirrored packet size: 'truncation <bytes>' captures only headers
Enable truncation to reduce mirrored packet size: 'truncation <bytes>' captures only headers
Enable truncation to reduce mirrored packet size: 'truncation <bytes>' captures only headers

root_causes.cause: Multiple source ports with high utilization all mirrored to a single lower-speed destination
Bidirectional mirroring (both rx and tx) doubles the mirrored traffic volume
No ingress or egress filter to limit mirrored traffic to only the flows of interest
Destination port speed is lower than aggregate source traffic (e.g., 1G destination for 10G sources)

failure_modes.scenario: SPAN oversubscription — mirrored traffic exceeds destination port capacity
symptoms: Drops or output discards on the SPAN destination port
Packet capture shows gaps, missing TCP segments, or retransmissions that don't exist on the source
Capture analysis shows asymmetric flows — one direction captured, other direction dropped
Wireshark reports 'previous segment not captured' or 'out of order' on the destination port

verify_commands.confirms_if: Non-zero and incrementing output drops on the destination port confirm SPAN oversubscription
verify_commands.look_for: Check output drops, output discards, and TX rate — high output drops confirm oversubscription

verify_commands.confirms_if: Non-zero and incrementing output drops on the destination port confirm SPAN oversubscription
verify_commands.look_for: Check output drops, output discards, and TX rate — high output drops confirm oversubscription

verify_commands.confirms_if: Non-zero and incrementing output drops on the destination port confirm SPAN oversubscription
verify_commands.look_for: Check output drops, output discards, and TX rate — high output drops confirm oversubscription

verify_commands.confirms_if: Multiple high-bandwidth source ports with 'both' direction mirrored to a single port confirms oversubscription risk
verify_commands.look_for: Count the number of source ports and check direction (both/rx/tx) to estimate aggregate mirrored bandwidth

verify_commands.confirms_if: Multiple high-bandwidth source ports with 'both' direction mirrored to a single port confirms oversubscription risk
verify_commands.look_for: Count the number of source ports and check direction (both/rx/tx) to estimate aggregate mirrored bandwidth

verify_commands.confirms_if: Multiple high-bandwidth source ports with 'both' direction mirrored to a single port confirms oversubscription risk
verify_commands.look_for: Count the number of source ports and check direction (both/rx/tx) to estimate aggregate mirrored bandwidth

failure_modes.description: Active SPAN or ERSPAN sessions are causing measurable performance degradation on the production switch. This can manifest as high CPU utilization, packet loss on non-SPAN ports, or increased latency. Root causes include ACL-based SPAN consuming TCAM resources, SPAN-to-CPU configurations that punt mirrored traffic to the control plane, too many active sessions exhausting ASIC mirroring resources, or ERSPAN encapsulation overhead impacting the data plane.

resolutions.action: Remove ACL-based SPAN sessions that are no longer needed to free TCAM
Remove ACL-based SPAN sessions that are no longer needed to free TCAM
Remove ACL-based SPAN sessions that are no longer needed to free TCAM
Avoid SPAN-to-CPU; use a physical destination port instead
Avoid SPAN-to-CPU; use a physical destination port instead
Avoid SPAN-to-CPU; use a physical destination port instead
Reduce the number of active SPAN sessions to the minimum required
Reduce the number of active SPAN sessions to the minimum required
Reduce the number of active SPAN sessions to the minimum required
Use traffic filters to limit mirrored volume and reduce ASIC load
Use traffic filters to limit mirrored volume and reduce ASIC load
Use traffic filters to limit mirrored volume and reduce ASIC load
Schedule SPAN sessions during maintenance windows for high-traffic sources
Schedule SPAN sessions during maintenance windows for high-traffic sources
Schedule SPAN sessions during maintenance windows for high-traffic sources
Monitor TCAM usage before and after enabling ACL-based SPAN
Monitor TCAM usage before and after enabling ACL-based SPAN
Monitor TCAM usage before and after enabling ACL-based SPAN

root_causes.cause: ACL-based SPAN consuming TCAM entries — reduces available TCAM for production ACLs and routing
SPAN to CPU (punt) — mirrored traffic is punted to the control plane, overwhelming CoPP and the CPU
Too many active SPAN sessions exhausting ASIC mirroring hardware resources
ERSPAN encapsulation/decapsulation overhead on the data plane
SPAN source is a high-traffic uplink — mirroring copies consume forwarding bandwidth

failure_modes.scenario: SPAN causing performance impact — CPU or ASIC impact from mirroring
symptoms: High CPU utilization on the switch correlated with SPAN session activation
Packet loss or increased latency on production (non-SPAN) ports
TCAM utilization increase after enabling ACL-filtered SPAN
Control plane policing (CoPP) drops increase when SPAN-to-CPU is active

verify_commands.look_for: Check for high CPU utilization and identify top processes — look for SPAN/mirror-related processes

verify_commands.look_for: Check for high CPU utilization and identify top processes — look for SPAN/mirror-related processes

verify_commands.confirms_if: High TCAM usage or exhausted mirror resources confirm SPAN is impacting platform capacity
verify_commands.look_for: Check TCAM utilization and mirroring hardware resource usage

verify_commands.look_for: Check TCAM utilization and mirroring hardware resource usage

verify_commands.look_for: Check TCAM utilization and mirroring hardware resource usage

verify_commands.look_for: Count the total number of active SPAN/ERSPAN sessions and their source scope

purpose: Traffic mirroring for packet capture and monitoring — SPAN mirrors locally, ERSPAN encapsulates in GRE for remote delivery
standard: IETF draft (ERSPAN Type II/III), Cisco proprietary (SPAN/RSPAN)

config_examples:
monitor session 1 type erspan-source
 source interface GigabitEthernet1/0/1 both
 destination
  erspan-id 100
  ip address 10.10.10.100
  origin ip address 10.1.1.1
 no shutdown

notes.note: 'monitor session <id> source/destination'. ERSPAN via 'monitor session <id> type erspan-source'. Catalyst 9K: max sessions vary by platform. Supports flow-based SPAN with 'sampler'. Truncation: 'monitor session <id> destination truncation <bytes>'.

config_examples:
monitor-session erspan1 ethernet
 destination interface tunnel-ip1
 source interface TenGigE0/0/0/1 rx

notes.note: SPAN via 'monitor-session <name>'. ERSPAN supported on NCS 5500 and Cisco 8000. 'ethernet-services access-list' for filtered SPAN. Per-NPU mirroring limitations.

config_examples:
monitor session 1 type erspan-source
  source interface Ethernet1/1 both
  destination ip 10.10.10.100
  erspan-id 100
  origin ip address 10.1.1.1
  no shut

notes.note: 'monitor session <id> type span/erspan-source/erspan-destination'. Max 2 local SPAN + 4 ERSPAN on most Nexus 9000. ERSPAN Type II/III supported. ACL filter: 'filter access-group <acl>'. SPAN on FEX ports has limitations.
'config mirror_session add' for SPAN. ERSPAN supported. Config in CONFIG_DB MIRROR_SESSION table. Limited to 1-2 sessions on most Cisco 8000-O platforms.
